title: Collapsible Potting Table
summary: A folding garden potting bench with a slatted top that drains — hinged legs let it flatten against a wall or go in the shed for winter.
description:
A potting bench earns its space in spring and is in the way the rest of the year, so this one folds flat. Hinged legs collapse it down to store against a wall or in a shed, and a slatted work surface lets soil and water fall through instead of pooling. It's all 1-by lumber and pocket-hole joinery, sized as a compact garden workstation.

Build the two side leg frames and the back leg frame first. The back frame includes an angled brace — the 12-1/2" 1x2 cut at 45° on non-parallel ends — that gives the folding geometry its rigidity when open. Take the source's flagged correction seriously: one diagram measurement is wrong, and the shelf 1x4s should be cut 37-1/8" to fit, not the printed number.

Since it lives outside, plank the top on a scrap board with a slight gap between boards — the gap drains water and lets the boards expand and contract without cupping. For the folding action, before you screw hinges on, stand the two sides to be hinged next to each other and test-fold with the hinge in hand to confirm you're mounting it on the correct face; getting a hinge backwards is the classic folding-furniture mistake. Use exterior screws and an outdoor finish.

tools.note: The 45° angle cuts on the back-leg brace.

materials.name: 1x2 board, 8 ft
materials.note: Legs and frames; one extra per step 7.

cutList.note: Cut to fit — corrects the diagram error.

steps.title: Build the side leg frames
steps.body: Build the two side leg frames with the jig set for 3/4" stock and 1-1/4" pocket screws. Make them match.

steps.title: Build the back leg frame
steps.body: Build the back leg frame to the given dimensions. The angled piece is the 12-1/2" 1x2 with 45° cuts — it braces the folding geometry. Pocket holes set for 3/4" stock, 1-1/4" screws.

steps.title: Start the bottom shelf
steps.body: Note the corrected length — the shelf frame 1x4s are 37-1/8", cut to fit, not the printed diagram number. Inset your 1x2s 3/4" from the ends of the 1x4s and attach with 1-1/4" screws and glue, two screws per shelf per side.

steps.title: Fill in the shelf boards
steps.body: Evenly space the remaining shelf boards, gluing and screwing each in place with two screws per board per side.

steps.title: Plank the draining top
steps.body: You can pocket-hole the top boards together, but planking them on a scrap board with a slight gap between boards is better for an outdoor piece — the gap drains water and lets the boards move. Arrange them inside the 1x6 frame.

steps.title: Hinge, test-fold, and finish
steps.body: Before screwing hinges on, stand the sides to be hinged next to each other and test-fold with the hinge in hand to confirm the correct orientation, then scribe the screw locations. Mount the hinges, sand, and apply an exterior finish. Now it folds flat for storage.

images.alt: Folding wooden potting bench with a slatted top
